features.name: battery
verbatim: The flap for the compartment that stores the swappable batteries feels a bit flimsy, though
the Yamaha Aerox E comes with two 1.5 kWh swappable battery packs, taking the total output to 3 kWh.
3kwh of Aerox E

features.name: braking
verbatim: Strong and progressive brakes are confidence-inspiring and offer excellent stopping power
the Aerox E comes with disc brakes at both the front and rear. There's a good amount of bite on both ends, and it kicks in progressively, which inspires confidence.
you also get ... single-channel ABS
The stopping power is impressive, and you also get single-channel ABS.
a rear disk brake setup on Aerox E

features.name: build quality
verbatim: Premium and solid build quality. Consistent fit & finish across the exteriors
The materials have a solid premium feel to them, and the level of fit and finish is consistent on the exteriors

features.name: colour options
verbatim: the white colourway is exclusive to the Aerox E, and it is the only colour option on sale

features.name: dimensions
verbatim: The wheelbase, however, has gone up by 10 mm and now measures 1,380 mm
feels smaller compared to Aerox

features.name: ergonomics
verbatim: Poor ergonomics, especially for taller riders. Seat height has gone up, and its sloping design makes you slide forward under hard braking

features.name: features
verbatim: Loaded with features such as a 5-inch TFT with smartphone connectivity, music controls, turn-by-turn navigation, three ride modes, traction control, single-channel ABS and a rear disc brake

features.name: handling
verbatim: One of the better-handling electric scooters out there. Doesn't feel cumbersome to manoeuver at slow speeds
the Aerox E is one of the better-handling electric scooters out there.
Cornering grip is impressive, as well, and the tyres don't lock up easily under hard braking either

features.name: performance
verbatim: Performance in Power mode with Boost engaged is exhilarating and addictive

features.name: price
verbatim: Expected to be priced higher compared to EC-06, which itself is priced at bit of a premium
Aerox E is likely to be priced between 2 to 2.5 lakh

features.name: range
verbatim: 3 kWh battery pack and an IDC-claimed range of 117 km are below par
The IDC-claimed range is 117 km.
Riding it for ~30 km, the majority of which was in Power mode, used up 50 % of the charge.

features.name: regenerative braking
verbatim: Regenerative braking isn’t very strong. You’ll need to use the mechanical brakes often
the regenerative braking is quite ineffective. Riding downhill in Aamby Valley almost felt like the scooter was coasting in neutral

features.name: ride modes
verbatim: The '10.0' highlighted in green indicates that the Boost mode is available for use for 10 seconds
the Aerox E features Boost mode, which provides an extra surge of power for 10 seconds, regardless of the riding mode you are in.
There are three ride modes on offer - Eco, Standard and Power
the Aerox E comes with three ride modes—Eco, Standard and Power—which alter the throttle response and top speed of the scooter.
To engage reverse mode, you need to press and hold the top part of the switch for a couple of seconds

features.name: ride quality
verbatim: Rear suspension is very stiff. Ride quality can feel jarring over bad roads

features.name: seat height
verbatim: One big change in terms of ergonomics is the increased seat height on the Aerox E compared to the regular Aerox

features.name: styling
verbatim: Retains the ICE Aerox's sharp, sporty styling and unique, imposing stance
imagine a scooter looking like a hawk on hunt, but moving like a pigeon

features.name: suspension
verbatim: The Yamaha Aerox E features the same suspension hardware as its ICE counterpart. You have conventional telescopic forks up front and dual shocks at the rear.
the rear is stiffly sprung, and if you are a lightweight rider, be prepared to be thrown off the seat when riding swiftly over bumps.
the front feels much better in terms of absorption, and I didn't feel many jerks on the handlebar

features.name: traction control
verbatim: you also get ... traction control

features.name: braking
verbatim: Yamaha can’t seem to find a way to fit rear discs on this scooter

features.name: enthusiast appeal
verbatim: it became the enthusiast's choice looking for a fun-to-ride ICE scooter

features.name: motor
verbatim: With R15's liquid-cooled engine at its heart, putting out 14.75 BHP and 13.9 Nm

features.name: performance
verbatim: a sports-maxi scooter with a sole focus on delivering the riding thrills while being unapologetically impractical
does a lot more justice to the performance scooter nameplate

features.name: practicality
verbatim: focuses more on all-around practicality

features.name: price
verbatim: EC-06, which itself is priced at bit of a premium

features.name: regenerative braking
verbatim: the regenerative braking is not as strong or effective as the EC-06.

features.name: ride modes
verbatim: the grunt over 20 km/h is relatively stronger than EC-06's Eco mode

features.name: battery capacity
verbatim: better 4.4kwh battery pack vs 3kwh of Aerox E

features.name: competitor models
verbatim: Once launched, the Aerox E is expected to rival performance electric scooters such as the ... TVS X

features.name: exterior design
verbatim: TVS X looks better than the Aerox, it has quite a modern sharp design

features.name: vehicle price
verbatim: TVS X is at ~2.6 lakh INR
